title: 21.William S. Hart and the Early Western Film
filename: 21.William S. Hart and the Early Western Film

lines.english: Lesson 21
lines.chinese: 第21课
lines.raw: Lesson 21 | 第21课

lines.english: William S.Hart and the early 'Western' film
lines.chinese: 威廉·S·哈特和早期“西部”片
lines.raw: William S.Hart and the early 'Western' film | 威廉·S·哈特和早期“西部”片

lines.english: How did William Hart's childhood prepare him for his acting role in Western films?
lines.chinese: 威廉·哈特的童年是怎样为他在西部片中的表演做准备的？
lines.raw: How did William Hart's childhood prepare him for his acting role in Western films? | 威廉·哈特的童年是怎样为他在西部片中的表演做准备的？

lines.english: William S.Hart was, perhaps, the greatest of all Western stars,
lines.chinese: 威廉·S·哈特也许是所有西部片明星中最伟大的。
lines.raw: William S.Hart was, perhaps, the greatest of all Western stars, | 威廉·S·哈特也许是所有西部片明星中最伟大的。

lines.english: for unlike Gary Cooper and John Wayne he appeared in nothing but Westerns.
lines.chinese: 因为他不像加里·库珀和约翰·韦恩那样，他只出演西部片。
lines.raw: for unlike Gary Cooper and John Wayne he appeared in nothing but Westerns. | 因为他不像加里·库珀和约翰·韦恩那样，他只出演西部片。

lines.english: From 1914 to 1924 he was supreme and unchallenged.
lines.chinese: 从1914到1924年，他独领风骚，无人能及。
lines.raw: From 1914 to 1924 he was supreme and unchallenged. | 从1914到1924年，他独领风骚，无人能及。

lines.english: It was Hart who created the basic formula of the Western film,
lines.chinese: 是哈特创造了西部片的基本模式，
lines.raw: It was Hart who created the basic formula of the Western film, | 是哈特创造了西部片的基本模式，

lines.english: and devised the protagonist he played in every film he made,
lines.chinese: 并设计了他所主演的每部影片中的主角，
lines.raw: and devised the protagonist he played in every film he made, | 并设计了他所主演的每部影片中的主角，

lines.english: the good-bad man, the accidental, noble outlaw,
lines.chinese: 亦正亦邪的好坏人，偶然成为的正直的歹徒，
lines.raw: the good-bad man, the accidental, noble outlaw, | 亦正亦邪的好坏人，偶然成为的正直的歹徒，

lines.english: or the honest, but framed cowboy, or the sheriff made suspect by vicious gossip;
lines.chinese: 或是诚实但遭诬陷的牛仔，或是因流言蜚语而受到怀疑的警长；
lines.raw: or the honest, but framed cowboy, or the sheriff made suspect by vicious gossip; | 或是诚实但遭诬陷的牛仔，或是因流言蜚语而受到怀疑的警长；

lines.english: in short, the individual in conflict with himself and his frontier environment.
lines.chinese: 简而言之，就是与自己和其边疆环境相冲突的个体。
lines.raw: in short, the individual in conflict with himself and his frontier environment. | 简而言之，就是与自己和其边疆环境相冲突的个体。

lines.english: Unlike most of his contemporaries in Hollywood,
lines.chinese: 与他在好莱坞的大多数同时代演员不同，
lines.raw: Unlike most of his contemporaries in Hollywood, | 与他在好莱坞的大多数同时代演员不同，

lines.english: Hart actually knew something of the old West.
lines.chinese: 哈特确实了解一些旧西部。
lines.raw: Hart actually knew something of the old West. | 哈特确实了解一些旧西部。

lines.english: He had lived in it as a child when it was already disappearing,
lines.chinese: 他小时候曾生活在那个已经逐渐消失的西部，
lines.raw: He had lived in it as a child when it was already disappearing, | 他小时候曾生活在那个已经逐渐消失的西部，

lines.english: and his hero was firmly rooted in his memories and experiences,
lines.chinese: 他的英雄人物深深扎根于他的记忆和经历中，
lines.raw: and his hero was firmly rooted in his memories and experiences, | 他的英雄人物深深扎根于他的记忆和经历中，

lines.english: and in both the history and the mythology of the vanished frontier,
lines.chinese: 以及在消逝边疆的历史和神话中。
lines.raw: and in both the history and the mythology of the vanished frontier, | 以及在消逝边疆的历史和神话中。

lines.english: And although no period or place in American history has been more absurdly romanticized,
lines.chinese: 尽管美国历史上没有任何一个时期或地方比西部更被荒谬地浪漫化，
lines.raw: And although no period or place in American history has been more absurdly romanticized, | 尽管美国历史上没有任何一个时期或地方比西部更被荒谬地浪漫化，

lines.english: myth and reality did join hands in at least one arena,
lines.chinese: 但神话和现实至少在一个领域联手了，
lines.raw: myth and reality did join hands in at least one arena, | 但神话和现实至少在一个领域联手了，

lines.english: the conflict between the individual and encroaching civilization.
lines.chinese: 那就是个体与入侵文明之间的冲突。
lines.raw: the conflict between the individual and encroaching civilization. | 那就是个体与入侵文明之间的冲突。

lines.english: Men accustomed to struggling for survival against the elements and Indians
lines.chinese: 习惯于与自然元素和印第安人斗争求生的人们，
lines.raw: Men accustomed to struggling for survival against the elements and Indians | 习惯于与自然元素和印第安人斗争求生的人们，

lines.english: were bewildered by politicians, bankers and businessmen,
lines.chinese: 被政客、银行家和商人搞得手足无措，
lines.raw: were bewildered by politicians, bankers and businessmen, | 被政客、银行家和商人搞得手足无措，

lines.english: and unhorsed by fences, laws and alien taboos.
lines.chinese: 被篱笆、法律和陌生的禁忌所限制。
lines.raw: and unhorsed by fences, laws and alien taboos. | 被篱笆、法律和陌生的禁忌所限制。

lines.english: Hart's good-bad man was always an outsider, always one of the disinherited,
lines.chinese: 哈特的“亦正亦邪的好坏人”总是一个局外人，一个被剥夺继承权的人，
lines.raw: Hart's good-bad man was always an outsider, always one of the disinherited, | 哈特的“亦正亦邪的好坏人”总是一个局外人，一个被剥夺继承权的人，

lines.english: and if he found it necessary to shoot a sheriff or rob a bank along the way,
lines.chinese: 如果他觉得有必要射杀一名警长或沿途抢劫一家银行，
lines.raw: and if he found it necessary to shoot a sheriff or rob a bank along the way, | 如果他觉得有必要射杀一名警长或沿途抢劫一家银行，

lines.english: his early audiences found it easy to understand and forgive,
lines.chinese: 他的早期观众很容易理解并原谅，
lines.raw: his early audiences found it easy to understand and forgive, | 他的早期观众很容易理解并原谅，

lines.english: especially when it was Hart who, in the end, overcame the attacking Indians.
lines.chinese: 尤其当最终是哈特击败了来犯的印第安人时。
lines.raw: especially when it was Hart who, in the end, overcame the attacking Indians. | 尤其当最终是哈特击败了来犯的印第安人时。

lines.english: Audiences in the second decade of the twentieth century
lines.chinese: 二十世纪第二个十年的观众
lines.raw: Audiences in the second decade of the twentieth century | 二十世纪第二个十年的观众

lines.english: found it pleasant to escape to a time when life, though hard, was relatively simple.
lines.chinese: 乐于逃到一个虽然艰辛但相对简单的时代。
lines.raw: found it pleasant to escape to a time when life, though hard, was relatively simple. | 乐于逃到一个虽然艰辛但相对简单的时代。

lines.english: We still do; living in a world in which undeclared aggression, war, hypocrisy,
lines.chinese: 我们现在仍然如此；生活在一个不宣而战的侵略、战争、虚伪、
lines.raw: We still do; living in a world in which undeclared aggression, war, hypocrisy, | 我们现在仍然如此；生活在一个不宣而战的侵略、战争、虚伪、

lines.english: chicanery, anarchy and impending immolation are part of our daily lives,
lines.chinese: 诡计、无政府状态和即将到来的毁灭是我们日常生活一部分的世界里，
lines.raw: chicanery, anarchy and impending immolation are part of our daily lives, | 诡计、无政府状态和即将到来的毁灭是我们日常生活一部分的世界里，

lines.english: we all want a code to live by.
lines.chinese: 我们都渴望有一个行为准则。
lines.raw: we all want a code to live by. | 我们都渴望有一个行为准则。
